cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: The CMS is becoming the AI operating system for brands
stories.plain: This piece argues that choosing a CMS now means choosing your AI infrastructure — for content discovery by AI engines, personalization, governance, and agentic workflows. It reframes a familiar buying decision as a high-stakes strategic choice, directly affecting how marketing teams should evaluate and build their stack.

stories.head: Why AI search makes trust your most important visibility signal
stories.plain: As AI assistants answer questions instead of listing links, brand authority and reputation become the new ranking factors. The article explains how AI systems evaluate trustworthiness and what technical and editorial moves marketers must make to stay visible inside AI-generated answers — a core concern for any brand's reach strategy.

stories.head: Attestiv Launches DeepScan for Deepfake and Document Validation
stories.plain: DeepScan uses forensic signals and configurable business rules to automatically flag manipulated photos, documents, audio, and video before they influence critical business decisions. As AI-generated media floods marketing and customer-service workflows, automated content authenticity checks are becoming a governance must-have rather than a nice-to-have.

stories.head: Akeneo Introduces the First Truly Agentic Product Experience Platform
stories.plain: Akeneo's Summer Release turns its product data cloud into a platform where fleets of coordinated agents manage and enrich product content autonomously. For commerce and marketing teams, this signals a shift from manually curated product catalogs to AI-driven product experience management, changing how product data is built and governed at scale.

stories.src: Agile Brand · via Agile Brand Guide
stories.head: Guideline Adds Verified, Transaction-Level Ad Measurement Across ChatGPT and Perplexity
stories.plain: Guideline now tracks verified transaction-level advertising measurement inside AI answer engines like ChatGPT and Perplexity. This is significant because it starts to close the attribution gap that has made AI-engine advertising hard to justify — giving buyers real ROI signals and accelerating budget shifts toward GEO-style placements.

stories.head: The CMS is becoming the AI operating system for brands
stories.plain: Choosing a content management system now means choosing infrastructure for AI discovery, agentic workflows, and personalization — not just publishing pages. This reframes a decision most marketers treat as IT plumbing into a strategic AI governance and visibility call that affects how customers find and experience a brand inside AI-powered search and chat.

stories.head: Why AI search makes trust your most important visibility signal
stories.plain: As AI engines like ChatGPT and Perplexity replace traditional search results pages, brand authority and reputation signals — not just keyword rankings — determine who gets surfaced. Marketers need to rethink measurement and content strategy around credibility rather than clicks, making this a practical GEO playbook shift.

stories.head: How AI discovery is changing everything marketers measure
stories.plain: AI-powered discovery is eroding the traffic and click metrics marketers have relied on for years. The article explains what to measure instead — share of AI citations, brand mention quality, and outcome-based signals — giving teams a concrete framework for adapting measurement when the path to purchase runs through an AI answer rather than a search results page.

stories.head: Akeneo Introduces the First Truly Agentic Product Experience Platform
stories.plain: Akeneo's Summer Release converts its Product Cloud from a data store into a fleet of coordinating agents that autonomously manage product content tasks. This matters because it moves product information management — a core content supply chain for e-commerce — from human-driven editing to agentic automation, changing how teams buy and govern the platform.

stories.head: The hidden cost of your fragmented martech stack
stories.plain: Best-of-breed tool collections create a structural complexity wall that slows revenue operations and makes AI integration harder. The piece gives practical language for quantifying hidden coordination costs — relevant to any team evaluating whether to consolidate onto platforms with built-in AI capabilities versus continuing to stitch point solutions together.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: The Agentic Layer Moves Into Production
body: Agentic AI has crossed from experimentation into deployment infrastructure. Alliances like Globant & Vercel's AI Pods, Akeneo's agentic product-experience platform, and Wistia's MCP server all share a common architecture: autonomous agents wired directly into live business systems, not sandboxed demos.

The practical implication is that marketing and product teams are being asked to make governance decisions — who controls the agent, what it can change — before the tooling for those decisions is mature. Speed of adoption is outrunning speed of guardrails.

themes.head: AI Search Visibility Rewrites the Measurement Playbook
body: As AI-powered discovery (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews) replaces traditional search ranking, the metrics marketers have trusted for a decade — clicks, impressions, keyword rank — are becoming structurally unreliable. Trust signals, brand citations, and entity authority are emerging as the new proxy for visibility.

Platforms like Scrunch are racing to quantify AI-surface presence, while editorial voices argue that trust is now the primary visibility signal in AI search. Marketers who wait for a consensus measurement standard risk becoming invisible in the channels where buying decisions are increasingly made.

themes.head: The CMS & Stack Are Consolidating Around AI
body: The content management system is being reframed not as a publishing tool but as the AI operating system that orchestrates brand content, data, and agent actions in one place. This mirrors a broader push — visible in HubSpot's Breeze upgrades and ActiveCampaign's Google Ads Connector — toward fewer, deeper platforms rather than sprawling point-solution stacks.

The hidden cost of fragmentation is getting explicit attention: redundant data pipelines, inconsistent customer context, and compounding technical debt. Consolidation is now a strategic argument, not just an IT efficiency play, because fragmented stacks actively degrade AI output quality.
ev: MarTech · hidden cost of fragmented stack

themes.head: AI ROI Accountability Becomes Non-Negotiable
body: A cluster of launches this month — TechSee's Value Meter, Pipedrive's clarity-over-automation report, and the broader revenue-driven marketing engine conversation — signals that enterprise buyers are demanding real-time, board-legible evidence of AI's business impact, not anecdotal productivity gains.

This is a maturation marker: AI tools are being held to the same ROI scrutiny as any other line-item spend. Vendors who cannot surface a clear value metric will lose deals to those who can, regardless of underlying capability.

themes.head: Trust & Authenticity Verification Enter the Ad Supply Chain
body: Deepfake detection is moving from a content-moderation curiosity into an operational layer of the ad supply chain. Attestiv's DeepScan extends verification to file-level assets, while HUMAN Security's takedown of the 'NewsJunkie' CTV spoofing operation shows that fraud is scaling with the same AI tools brands are using to create content.

The convergence of synthetic media risk and programmatic ad fraud means that authenticity verification is becoming infrastructure — a requirement woven into buying platforms and asset pipelines, not an optional add-on. Brands that ignore this face both wasted spend and reputational exposure.
ev: MarTech Series · customer comms & regulatory change

month.takeaway: Across every layer of the stack — content, search, agents, measurement, and supply-chain integrity — AI is forcing marketers to make architectural decisions that will be very hard to reverse, making the next six months disproportionately consequential for long-term competitive position.
